improvement_bullets: Designed and implemented a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting Federal Reserve economic data into Google BigQuery, automating data quality validation with Great Expectations and deploying CI/CD via GitHub Actions to ensure data integrity and adherence to research standards.

Developed robust data models and ETL pipelines using Python and dbt, incorporating dimensional modeling principles (star schema) and BigQuery optimization techniques (partitioning, clustering) to create research-ready datasets, demonstrating an understanding of scalable data architectures.

Extracted and transformed data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured, clean datasets that directly supported front-end feature development and utilized SQL queries to validate and synchronize data across systems, improving data accuracy by 60%, aligning with the need for research-ready datasets.

Collaborated with stakeholders to align software features with business processes and objectives, reducing product delivery delays by 25%, and worked with engineers and product managers to translate user feedback into production code, driving a 15% increase in feature adoption, demonstrating experience in translating complex needs into scalable solutions.

Automated recurring business operations and reporting workflows using workflow automation tools, reducing manual tasks by 30%, which demonstrates experience in building automated data pipelines and improving operational efficiency, relevant to research data automation.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

i am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with Jobs via Dice. I am interested in this opportunity because Montefiore Medical Center's commitment to advancing medicine and its national recognition in clinical excellence and research deeply resonates with my passion for leveraging data to drive scientific discovery and improve patient outcomes.

What interests me about working for Montefiore is its approach to integrating clinical care and scientific discovery. This intersection excites, inspires, and intrigues me because it aligns with my goal of building robust data infrastructure that supports critical research use cases.

I believe my proficiency in SQL and my practical experience with Snowflake and BigQuery would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on building scalable and reliable data solutions. In fact, in my FRED Economic Batch Pipeline project, I designed and implemented a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting Federal Reserve economic data into Google BigQuery using Python, Airflow, dbt, and GCS. I modeled raw API data into a dbt star schema, reducing analyst query time via BigQuery partitioning and clustering. I also automated data quality validation with Great Expectations and deployed a CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Jobs via Dice would allow me to bring my expertise in data pipeline development and data modeling to support key responsibilities such as designing, building, and automating robust, scalable data pipelines for ingesting, transforming, and loading diverse healthcare and research data.

Additionally, my experience as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my skills in translating complex needs into technical solutions. In that role, I collaborated with stakeholders to align software features with business processes and objectives, reducing product delivery delays by 25%. I also extracted and transformed data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured, clean datasets that directly supported front-end feature development and improved data accuracy by 60%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my data transformation and integration skills in working on the integration and harmonization of data across heterogeneous sources at Montefiore.

I know that my SQL proficiency and my experience with modern data architectures align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
